description: M8 Customisation + Polish — VS Code themes, pinned themes, settings import, live status bar, usage charts, onboarding flow

userStories.title: Add bundled VS Code themes to theme registry
userStories.description: As a user, I want the 7 bundled themes listed in the PRD available out of the box (PRD US-17).
acceptanceCriteria: Add theme JSON files for: Dracula, Catppuccin Mocha, Tokyo Night, Solarized Dark, Solarized Light (GitHub Dark and One Dark Pro should already exist)
Each theme file stored in src/themes/data/
Update theme registry to include all 7 bundled themes
Theme switcher dropdown shows all 7 options
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add pinned themes to left toolbar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pin up to 4 themes to the left toolbar for instant switching (PRD US-22).
acceptanceCriteria: Add pinnedThemes array to layout store (max 4 theme IDs)
Pinned themes appear as small color swatch circles below the standard toolbar items
Clicking a pinned theme applies it instantly
Add pin/unpin controls to Settings → Themes tab
Pinned state persists in layout store
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Connect status bar to live backend metrics
userStories.description: As a user, I want the status bar to show real CPU, RAM, token count, and cost from the backend (PRD US-23).
acceptanceCriteria: Add GET /api/system/metrics endpoint returning { cpu: number, ram: number, uptime: number }
CPU: percentage via process.cpuUsage(), RAM: process.memoryUsage().rss in MB
Add GET /api/usage/session endpoint returning { totalTokens: number, estimatedCost: number }
Update StatusBar.tsx to fetch metrics every 2 seconds via polling
Replace placeholder values with real data
Session timer already works — keep it
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create usage tracking database table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a table to store token usage and cost data per agent/stage.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/db/schema/usage.ts with id, workspaceId, projectId, pipelineId, stageId, agentName, model, provider, inputTokens, outputTokens, toolCalls, estimatedCost, createdAt
Update barrel export in schema/index.ts
Migration generates and runs successfully
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create usage charts REST API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need API endpoints for usage data visualization (PRD US-13).
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/routes/usage.ts as a Fastify plugin
GET /api/usage/summary returns total tokens, estimated cost, most active agent, most used model for current session
GET /api/usage/timeline returns time-series data grouped by provider/model
GET /api/usage/breakdown returns grouped data by provider, model, agent, project
GET /api/usage/export?format=csv returns CSV export of usage data
Register routes in server/index.ts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Build usage charts UI in bottom panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see token, cost, and tool usage charts in the Usage Graphs tab (PRD US-13).
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/api/usage.ts with useUsageSummary(), useUsageTimeline(), useUsageBreakdown() hooks
Update BottomPanel Usage Graphs tab with real data
Summary cards: total tokens, estimated cost, most active agent, most used model
Replace hardcoded usage data with API data
Add CSV export button calling /api/usage/export?format=csv
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Full interactive charts (zoomable timeline, bar graphs) deferred — v1 uses summary cards and a simple table view.

userStories.title: Add VS Code settings import
userStories.description: As a user, I want to import my VS Code keyboard shortcuts and editor settings (PRD US-18).
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'Import' tab to SettingsView
File upload input for keybindings.json and settings.json
Parse keybindings.json and display imported shortcuts in a table
Parse settings.json and extract: font size, font family, tab size, insert spaces preference
Apply imported settings to Insomniac settings store
Show 'Unmapped shortcuts' section for VS Code shortcuts with no Insomniac equivalent
Non-destructive: Insomniac defaults retained for anything not in the VS Code config
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create onboarding flow
userStories.description: As a new user, I want a guided setup when I first open Insomniac (PRD Section 9).
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/onboarding/OnboardingWizard.tsx
Step 1: Welcome screen with tagline 'Insomniac is an AI developer console. You direct. Agents build.'
Step 2: Connect a provider — show provider form (reuse from SettingsView), at least one required
Step 3: Connect GitHub — optional, shows GitHub URL input
Step 4: Create first project — from prompt, template, or GitHub repo
Step 5: Done — show confirmation and redirect to main view
Show onboarding on first launch (check settings for 'onboarding_completed' key)
Skip button available on each step
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Connect Admin Terminal to real orchestrator logs
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Admin Terminal to show real streaming logs from the orchestrator.
acceptanceCriteria: Create server/routes/logs.ts with GET /api/logs endpoint returning recent log entries from SQLite
Add a logs table to store orchestrator/agent/system log entries (id, source, level, message, createdAt)
WebSocket events ('log:entry') stream new log entries in real time
Update BottomPanel Admin Terminal tab to show real logs from the API
Color-code logs by source (orchestrator=green, agent=blue, system=gray, error=red)
Add search/filter input for log entries
Remove hardcoded terminal log data
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Update active agents sidebar with real data
userStories.description: As a user, I want the right sidebar to show real active agents from the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/api/agents.ts with useActiveAgents() hook
Add GET /api/agents/active endpoint returning currently running agents with task and progress
Update RightSidebar 'Active agents' section to use real API data
Remove hardcoded AGENTS array
Show agent name, current task, model, progress bar, blocked/running status
Updates via WebSocket ('agent:status') events
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
